expectations.evidence: All 8 files present: design.md (583 lines), security-design.md (253 lines), api-spec.md (878 lines), database-schema.md (408 lines), architecture-decisions.md (342 lines), traceability.md (135 lines), test-impact.md (321 lines), test-specifications.md (1076 lines). Total: 3996 lines.

expectations.text: Component and sequence diagrams use Mermaid (no ASCII art)
expectations.evidence: 10 Mermaid diagram blocks found across design.md (8) and database-schema.md (2). Includes component diagram, 5 sequence diagrams, deployment diagram, and 2 ER diagrams. No ASCII art.

All 6 STRIDE categories found in security-design.md: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege. 73 total threats identified across 12 components.

expectations.text: STRIDE applied to every component and data flow
expectations.evidence: 12 components analyzed: Auth Middleware, SSO Integration, Task CRUD, Board Management, Membership Management, Webhook Config, Webhook Delivery, Search, Activity Log, Rate Limiter, Validation MW, Task Repository. 7/12 have full 6/6 coverage; 5 have justified gaps for non-applicable categories.

expectations.text: Every STRIDE threat has severity + mitigation strategy
expectations.evidence: 91 severity references (Critical/High/Medium/Low) and 12 explicit mitigation sections found. Every threat row in the table includes severity column and mitigation strategy column.

expectations.text: Every US-* maps to >= 1 API endpoint
expectations.evidence: 28 US-XXX-XXX references in api-spec.md. All 16 user stories (US-001-001 through US-005-003) mapped to 17 endpoints. QG confirmed zero orphan stories.

8 ADRs found (ADR-001 through ADR-008): Framework, Auth, Soft Delete, Webhook Delivery, Search, ORM, Retention, Rate Limiting. 17 'alternative' references — each ADR has 3-4 alternatives. All exceed >= 2 threshold.

expectations.text: ITS-* integration test specs for API endpoints
expectations.evidence: 60 ITS-* specs found in test-specifications.md covering all API endpoints with preconditions, request, expected response, and assertions.

expectations.text: STS-* system test specs for architecture flows
expectations.evidence: 8 STS-* specs found covering multi-step architecture flows: task lifecycle, webhook delivery with retry, search pipeline, RBAC enforcement, board management, activity log, rate limiting, health monitoring.

expectations.text: SECTS-* security test specs for STRIDE findings
expectations.evidence: 10 SECTS-* specs found covering JWT forgery, token replay, SQL injection, IDOR, webhook SSRF, XSS, role escalation, data leakage, DoS, audit tampering.

design/traceability.md contains complete mapping chain. QG confirmed all cells filled with no orphans. Chain: Requirement -> Story -> API Endpoint -> Data Model -> Component -> STRIDE Threat -> ADR -> Test Scope.
quality-gates/qg-design.yaml created by separate quality-gate-validator agent. Contains quality_gate_id: QG-DES-001, verdict: PASS, 8 criteria evaluated (7 PASS, 1 WARN for minor STRIDE gaps).

knowledge/design-capture.yaml created with KC-DESIGN-TASKFLOW-20260307 ID. 13 learnings across 7 categories (architecture, security, api_design, data_modeling, decisions, testing). 5 implementation recommendations.

expectations.text: Traceability-analyst updated matrix with design mappings
expectations.evidence: traceability.yaml updated: phases_covered expanded to [requirements, design]. 252 total links (up from 69). API endpoints, components, STRIDE threats, test specs all linked. Design coverage: 100%, 0 orphans.

summary: 14/14 assertions passed. Full 6-Activity Anatomy executed for Design phase. 8 design files (3996 lines), 10 Mermaid diagrams, 73 STRIDE threats, 8 ADRs, 78 test specs (60 ITS + 8 STS + 10 SECTS), separate QG (PASS), traceability + KC completed.
